prep.summary: Suhas has recently engaged you on process observability via Celonis—specifically validating its integration with Skan.ai for the P&C scorecard—and SMS risk assessment for the upcoming November claims release. Additionally, the new tech procurement process from June 23 establishes architects as early approval authorities—meaning this sync likely triggers your new 'Go TPDT' pre-approval responsibilities. You must clarify if Suhas's invite concerns these new procurement rules, the pending Celonis observability position paper, or the 2-way SMS risk mitigation plan.
tasks: Confirm if the Celonis position paper has been reviewed by leadership.
Finalize the template and control guidelines for the November claims release to mitigate PII risks.
Review the 'Technology Engagement in Procurement' wiki to verify architect approval steps before the sync.
Address the unresolved integration strategy between Celonis, Skan.ai, and Snowflake.
prep.title: Architecture Sync with Suhas

events.summary: Monthly EA Touchpoint with Ian Benoist

prep.summary: Review the critical outcomes from your recent tech decision syncs—particularly the June 24 sessions with Dan and Thomas—to address mainframe data offloading and architectural governance. Establish the transition of IFR, ECPA, and policy summary databases to a Redis and Pub/Sub model—this eliminates the stale data risks that leadership questioned. Resolve the ongoing governance friction between the CTO and CIO teams—Thomas requires your active advocacy to prevent Dan's group from bypassing established architectural channels.
tasks: Validate the Redis and Pub/Sub architectural design—specifically the stale data indicator—before offloading the IFR and ECPA databases from the mainframe.
Reinforce the CTO team's mandate in your upcoming sync with Dan—preventing the CIO team from bypassing established architectural channels.
Resolve the compliance and technical blockages regarding the proposed "splitter" A/B testing concept.
Formulate the migration strategy for moving policy summary database workloads off the mainframe to reduce MIPS usage.
prep.title: Quick Sync on Tech Decisions
